---
title: "Apply genomic prediction"
author: Charlotte Brault
output: html_document
df_print: kable
date: '`r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE,
                      engine.opts = list(bash = "-l"))
```

# Libraries

```{r load libraries}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(here)) # root directory
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(data.table)) # load data
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(apercu)) # ap
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(rrBLUP)) # genomic selection
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr)) # formatting
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggplot2)) # plot
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(paletteer)) # plot palette
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(janitor)) # formating
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggvenn)) # Venn diagram
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(glmnet)) # LASSO
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(doParallel)) # for parallelization
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(foreach)) # for parallelization
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(snow)) # for creating cluster
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(doSNOW)) # for creating cluster
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(BGLR)) # Bayes
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(caret)) # method cross val
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(purrr)) # list handling
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(bigstatsr)) # ggplot theme
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggbeeswarm)) # ggplot geom_quasirandom
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(gtsummary)) # print summary table
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(asreml)) # print summary table#devtools::install_github("chabrault/HRSWGS")
#suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(HRSWGS)) ## custom functions

nb.cores <- 10
```


# Set up root directory

```{r current dir}
here::i_am("analysis/genom_pred.Rmd")
```


This R chunk is used to assess how much time it takes to execute the R code in this document until the end:

```{r time_0}
t0 <- proc.time()
```



## Phenotypic data


The phenotypic data is the curated data for the SDSU breeding program. We extracted the BLUPs in the script `format_pheno_statgenSTA.Rmd`.

```{r load_pheno_trials}
## load raw data and get genotype BLUEs
# p2f <- here("output",paste0("BLUPs_6traits_",pop_code.trial,"_statgenSTA-",
#                             par.gv,".tsv"))
p2f <- here("data", "phenotypic", "allAccessions_yield_raw.csv")
pheno <- fread(p2f)
dim(pheno)
head(pheno)
#(ngeno <- length(intersect(pheno$GID,rownames(geno))))
#setdiff(pheno$GID, rownames(geno))
str(pheno)

print(length(unique(pheno$germplasmName)))
print(length(unique(pheno$studyDbId)))

# any NAs in yield 
colSums(is.na(pheno))

## I extracted only three columns for data exploration
# Keep only what we need, with concise names
ph <- as.data.table(pheno)[, .(
  gid  = germplasmName,
  env  = studyDbId,
  value
)]
ph[, value := as.numeric(value)]

# experiment/env specific stats
env_stats <- ph[, .(
  n    = .N,
  mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd   = sd(value,   na.rm = TRUE),
  max  = max(value,  na.rm = TRUE),   
  min = min(value,  na.rm = TRUE)  
), by = env][order(env)]

print(env_stats)


p1 <- ggplot(env_stats, aes(mean)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "lightgray", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of environment means",
       x = "Env mean (yield)", y = "Count") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)

p1



p2 <- ggplot(env_stats, aes(mean, sd, color = n)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  labs(title = "Environment spread vs level",
       x = "Env mean", y = "Env SD", color = "n (plots)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)
p2


### adding CV and Se t =o the table
setDT(env_stats)
# Derived metrics
env_stats[, `:=`(
  cv   = 100 * sd / mean,                 # coefficient of variation (%)
  se   = sd / sqrt(pmax(n, 1))           # standard error of env mean
)]


# flag the env based on cv, se, or less number of plots  (we can adjust the limits)
env_stats[, flag := fifelse(n < 15, "low_n",
                      fifelse(cv > 30, "high_cv",
                      fifelse(sd == 0, "zero_sd", NA_character_)))]

env_stats[, n_flags := (!is.na(flag)) + 0L]

print(env_stats)
fwrite(env_stats, here::here("output", "rawData_summaryByenv.csv"))

suspected_errors <- env_stats[
  n < 15 | cv > 30 | sd == 0
][order(-n_flags, -cv)]

fwrite(suspected_errors, here::here("output", "env_suspects_review.csv"))
print(suspected_errors)


## Connectivity
setDT(ph)
ph_small <- unique(ph[, .(env = factor(env), gid = factor(gid))])  

env_ix <- as.integer(ph_small$env)
gid_ix <- as.integer(ph_small$gid)
n_env  <- nlevels(ph_small$env)
n_gid  <- nlevels(ph_small$gid)

# Sparse incidence matrix: rows = env, cols = gid; 1 if tested
M <- sparseMatrix(i = env_ix, j = gid_ix, x = 1, dims = c(n_env, n_gid),
                  dimnames = list(levels(ph_small$env), levels(ph_small$gid)))

C_counts <- M %*% t(M)                    
C_counts <- as.matrix(C_counts)
C_counts



```

<!-- Add FHB traits, analyzed in `format_pheno_FHB.Rmd`. -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- p2f <- here("data","phenotypic",paste0("FHBMTtrials_2018-2024_adjusted_pheno_", -->
<!--                                        "5traits.tsv")) -->
<!-- file.mtime(p2f) -->
<!-- phenoFHB <- read.table(file=p2f, header=T, sep="\t") -->
<!-- dim(phenoFHB) -->
<!-- pheno <- pheno %>%  -->
<!--   left_join(phenoFHB, by="GID") %>% -->
<!--   select(GID, everything()) %>% -->
<!--   relocate(GID) -->
<!-- ``` -->

Export combined phenotypic data
```{r}
p2f <- here("data","phenotypic",
            paste0(pop_code.trial,"-BLUPs_",c(ncol(pheno)-1),"traits.tsv"))
write.table(pheno, file=p2f, sep="\t", quote=FALSE, row.names=FALSE)
tools::md5sum(p2f)
```


# Load data

## Genomic data

Load the cleaned and imputed genomic data, filtered for minor allele frequency at 5%.
File generated in `combine_format_vcf`.

```{r load_geno}
ninds.geno <- 1679 ; pop_code.geno <- "MSU2022-MSU2025-W3K"

p2f.geno <- here("data","genotypic", "merged_3K",
                 paste0(pop_code.geno, "_genoMatrix_",ninds.geno,"inds.rds"))
file.mtime(p2f.geno)
geno <- readRDS(p2f.geno)
dim(geno)
geno.init <- geno
geno <- janitor::remove_constant(geno)
geno[,2:ncol(geno)] <- scale(geno[,2:ncol(geno)], center=T, scale=T)
ap(geno)
gid.geno <- rownames(geno)
```



## Genotype information

Genotype information was generated in `explore_format_pheno.Rmd`, and from the input of `combine_format_vcf.Rmd`.
```{r}
geno.info <- fread(here("data","MSU2022-MSU2025-W3K_genotype_info.tsv"),data.table=F)
head(geno.info)
dim(geno.info)
```


# Genomic prediction

Cross-validation parameters:
```{r CV_params}
dim(pheno) ## 1126 x 12
stopifnot(!any(duplicated(pheno$GID)))
## params:
ar <- "3k" ## genotypic array
nreps <- 10 ## number of cross-validation repetitions
nfolds <- 10 ## number of cross-validation folds
nIter <- 10000 ## default 6000
burnIn <- 2000 ## default 1000
h <- 1 ## bandwidth parameter for RKHS
rerun <- TRUE ## set to FALSE to avoid rerunning genomic prediction
ntraits <- ncol(pheno)-1
(traits <- colnames(pheno)[-1]) ## verify that the trait names are correct
## list of possible GP methods
methods <- c("rrBLUP","GBLUP","RKHS")#,"GBLUP",RKHS-KA","BayesA","BayesB","RandomForest","LASSO")
nmeths <- length(methods)
```

Apply genomic prediction for all methods and traits.

Description of the parameters:

* **geno**: genotype matrix, with individuals as rows and markers as columns. It is better to scale the genotype matrix.

* **pheno**: phenotype matrix, with individuals as rows and traits as columns, genotype name must be in a column named "GID".

* **traits**: vector of the names of the traits to predict.

* **GP.method**: method to use for genomic prediction. It can be one of the following: "rrBLUP", "RKHS", "RKHS-KA", "BayesA", "BayesB", "RandomForest", "LASSO".

* **nreps**: number of repetitions of the cross-validation.

* **nfolds**: number of folds of the cross-validation.

* **p2d.temp**: path to the directory where the temporary files will be saved for computing Bayesian methods.

* **h**: bandwidth for the RKHS method. It can be a single value or a vector of values to test.

* **p2f.stats**: path to the file where the predictive ability results will be saved.

* **nb.cores**: number of cores to use for parallelization.

```{r, apply_gp_all}
gp.stats <- data.frame()
#pop <- paste0(pop_code.geno,"_",pop_code.trial,"_statgenSTA_",par.gv)
pop <- paste0(pop_code.trial,"_BLUPs")
for(meth in methods){
  
  p2f.stats <- here("output","genom_pred",
                    paste0(pop,"_GP_",meth,"_results_",ntraits, "traits_intraCV.tsv"))
  if(!file.exists(p2f.stats) |rerun){
    write(paste(meth,"\n"),stderr())
    out <- list()
    out[[meth]] <-  compute_GP_methods(geno=geno, pheno=pheno,
                                       traits=traits, GP.method=meth,
                                       nreps=nreps, nfolds=nfolds, 
                                       p2d.temp=here::here("output","genom_pred","temp"),
                                       h=h,p2f.stats=p2f.stats, 
                                       nb.cores=nb.cores)
    
    
    
    #gp.statsT <- purrr::map_dfr(outT, function(x) x$gp.stats)
    gp.stats <- rbind(gp.stats,out[[meth]]$gp.stats)
    
  } else{
    gp.stats <- rbind(gp.stats, read.table(file=p2f.stats, sep="\t", header=TRUE))
    
  }
}

ap(gp.stats)
tapply(gp.stats$corP, gp.stats$trait, mean, na.rm=T)
```

Explore predicted vs. observed values

```{r}
if(exists("out")){
  obspred <- do.call(rbind,out[[1]]$obspred$YIELD)
  
  obspred <- obspred %>% group_by(GID) %>% 
    summarise(obs=mean(obs), pred=mean(pred))
  #left_join(geno.info, by=GID)
  plotly::ggplotly(ggplot(obspred, aes(x=obs, y=pred,label=GID))+
                     geom_point(alpha=0.6, size=1) + 
                     geom_smooth(method="lm", se=FALSE, color="black")+
                     geom_rug(sides="bl")+
                     paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d("ggthemes::Jewel_Bright") +
                     ggpubr::stat_cor(method="pearson", size=3)+
                     geom_abline(intercept=0, slope=1, color="red")+
                     ggtitle("Predicted vs. observed values for MSU data",
                             subtitle=paste0(methods[1]," genomic selection method"))+
                     theme_bigstatsr(size.rel=0.8))
}

```



# Combine all 

```{r}
all.res <- gp.stats
dim(all.res)
head(all.res)
all.res$GP.method <- factor(all.res$GP.method, levels=methods)
purrr::map_dbl(all.res, n_distinct)
tapply(all.res$corP, all.res$GP.method, mean)

tapply(all.res$corP, all.res$trait, mean)


summ.corP= all.res %>% 
  group_by(trait) %>% summarise(meanCorP=mean(corP))

print_table(summ.corP)
ntraits <- length(traits)
##export the result table
p2f <- here("output","genom_pred",
            paste0("GP_",nmeths,"meths_PredAb_",ntraits,"traits_intraCV.tsv"))
write.table(all.res, file=p2f, sep="\t", quote=FALSE, row.names=FALSE)
```


# Plot

```{r}

# ggplot(all.res, aes(x=trait, y=corP, color=GP.method))+
#   facet_wrap(~pop)+
#   geom_boxplot() + theme_bw()
CPCOLS <- c("#008B00", "#EE82EE","red","lightseagreen", "#8B4500", "coral", "#4169E1")

ggplot(all.res,
       aes(y=trait, x=corP, color=GP.method))+
  #geom_boxplot(position=position_dodge(0.9)) +
  stat_summary(position=position_dodge(0.9),linewidth=1.2)+
  geom_point(alpha=0.3,position=position_dodge(0.9), size=1) +
  #theme_bw() + 
  theme_bigstatsr(size.rel=0.8)+
  xlab("Pearson correlation")+
  ggtitle("Predictive ability for MSU data",
          subtitle=paste0(nmeths," methods 3K genotype array, 10-folds CV"))+
  scale_color_manual(values=CPCOLS, name="GP methods")+
  geom_hline(yintercept =seq(1.5,length(unique(all.res$trait))-0.5,1))+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0,1,0.1), limits=c(0,1))+
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=0),
        text=element_text(size=13),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
        legend.position="bottom")
ggsave(file=here("output","figures",
                 paste0("GP_PredAb_",nmeths,"meths_",ntraits,"traits_intraCV.png")),
       height=8,width=11, scale=0.8)
```

# Predict genotypic values

Train the genomic selection model on the whole population using RKHS method and predict all the genotypic values.

## Model training

```{r pred_all_pop}
sel.meth <- "RKHS" ; h <- 1 ## bandwidth parameter for RKHS

p2d.temp=here::here("output","genom_pred","temp")
geno <- as.matrix(geno)
## create the distance matrix (a bit similar to the GRM) for RKHS method
D <- as.matrix(dist(geno,method="euclidean"))^2
D <- D/mean(D, na.rm=T)
### compute kernel
K <- exp(-h*D)
dim(K)
ap(K)


all.pred <- data.frame()
for(tr in traits){
  write(tr,stderr())
  
  ## combine phenotypic data
  # pheno <- rbind(phenoN[!phenoN$GID %in% cmon.pheno,c("GID",tr)],
  #                phenoT[,c("GID",tr)])
  ## estimate marker effects on the whole population
  yNA <- merge(data.frame(ind=rownames(geno)),
               pheno[,c("GID",tr)], by.x="ind", by.y="GID", all.x=TRUE)
  yNA <- yNA[[tr]]
  p2f.temp <- paste0(p2d.temp,"/RKHS_all_",tr)
  
  fit <- BGLR::BGLR(y=yNA,ETA=list(list(K=K,model='RKHS')),
                    nIter=nIter,burnIn=burnIn,saveAt=p2f.temp,verbose=FALSE)
  #as.data.frame(fit$yHat)
  tmp <- data.frame(GID=rownames(geno), trait=tr, yHat=fit$yHat)
  all.pred <- rbind(all.pred, tmp)
}

## add genotype information
all.pred <- merge(all.pred, geno.info, by="GID",all.x=T, all.y=F)

```



## Explore the prediction

Plot distribution
```{r plot_distrib}
# all.pred$FIRST_YEAR[is.na(all.pred$FIRST_YEAR)] <- "Unknown"
# ggplot(all.pred, aes(x=FIRST_YEAR, y=yHat, color=FIRST_YEAR))+
#   facet_wrap(~trait, scales="free_y")+
#   geom_quasirandom(alpha=0.3,size=1, color="grey85") + 
#   geom_boxplot(fill=NA) +
#   paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d("rcartocolor::Prism")+
#   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 19)))+
#   ggtitle("Genotypic value distribution",
#           subtitle="RKHS genomic selection method")+
#   ylab("Predicted genotypic value")+xlab("First year")+
#   theme_bigstatsr(size.rel=0.8)+ 
#   guides(color=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=19,size=2),nrow=2))+
#   scale_x_discrete(labels = scales::label_wrap(10))+
#   theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(),
#         legend.position="inside",
#         legend.title=element_blank(),
#         legend.position.inside=c(0.65,0.12))
# 
# 
# p2f <- here("output","figures",
#             paste0(pop,"_GP_PredVal_Distrib_",ntraits,"traits_wholeTrialPopGSTrain.png"))
# ggsave(file=p2f,height=10,width=13, scale=0.8)

```



Transform to a wide format

```{r}
info.cols <- colnames(geno.info)

all.pred.wide <- all.pred %>% 
  group_by(across(all_of(c(info.cols,"trait")))) %>%
  summarize(yHat=mean(yHat, na.rm=T),.groups="drop") %>%
  #select(all_of(c("geno","trait","pheno","Pedigree","Parent1","Parent2","EXPT","yHat")))%>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from="trait", values_from="yHat")
head(all.pred.wide)



p2f <- here("output","genom_pred",
            paste0(pop_code.geno,"_GP_PredVal_",sel.meth,"_",
                   ntraits,"traits.tsv"))
write.table(all.pred.wide, file=p2f, row.names=F, sep="\t", col.names=T)


# Get the summary table for all traits
gtsummary::tbl_summary(all.pred.wide, include=all_of(traits), by=FIRST_YEAR,
                       type = all_continuous() ~ "continuous2",
                       statistic = all_continuous() ~ c(
                         "{median} ({p25}, {p75})",
                         "{min}, {max}"
                       ))
#add_p(pvalue_fun = label_style_pvalue(digits = 2))
## print the highest 50 genotypic values for yield
all.pred.wide %>% arrange(desc(YIELD)) %>% head(200) %>% print_table()

```



## Export observed and predicted values

```{r export_obs_pred}
## combine all phenotypic data
all.pheno <- pheno
dim(all.pheno)
obs.pred <- merge(all.pheno, all.pred.wide, by="GID", all=TRUE,suffixes=c(".Obs",".Pred"))
obs.pred <- relocate(obs.pred, all_of(info.cols))
## put observed and predicted values side by side
obs.pred <- reduce(.x = traits,.f = ~ relocate(.x, starts_with(.y)),.init = obs.pred) |> 
  relocate(all_of(info.cols))

## put at first columns identifying the genotype
obs.pred <- relocate(obs.pred, GID) |> distinct() |> 
  group_by(across(all_of(info.cols))) |>
  summarize(across(where(is.numeric), ~ mean(.x, na.rm=T)),.groups="drop") |> 
  arrange(desc(YIELD.Pred))
## print first 100 rows
print_table(head(obs.pred, n=100))

## remove some decimals to numeric columns
obs.pred <- obs.pred |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, digits=2)))

p2f <- here("output", paste0(pop_code.geno,"_GP_ObsPredVal_",ntraits,"traits.tsv"))
write.table(obs.pred, file=p2f, row.names=F, sep="\t", col.names=T)
```


# Appendix

```{r info}
t1 <- proc.time(); t1 - t0
print(sessionInfo(), locale=FALSE)
```
